description: Gout / serum-urate panel: ABCG2 Q141K (rs2231142; urate-transporter risk allele T on the GRCh37 plus strand, 23andMe-plus = TT homozygous risk) + SLC2A9 (rs13129697; urate-raising allele T, the common allele; G is urate-lowering). ABCG2 gout odds are larger in Asian-ancestry meta-analysis strata (selected via odds_ratio_by_ancestry). Common risk alleles, not ClinVar P/LP -> clinvar_significance=NULL. Risk modifier, not a diagnosis; no dietary/treatment recommendations. EXPANSION_STRATEGY.md §9 / roadmap #43.

odds_ratio_by_ancestry.EAS: Gout odds ratio ~4.53 for the homozygous Q141K genotype (TT vs GG), using Asian-ancestry pooled genotype estimates
odds_ratio_by_ancestry.AFR: African-ancestry evidence is sparse; a meta-analysis estimated ~1.76x gout odds per Q141K risk allele, and homozygous Q141K is rare in African ancestry
odds_ratio_by_ancestry.default: Gout odds ratio ~3.24 for the homozygous Q141K genotype (TT vs GG), using Caucasian/European pooled genotype estimates

genotype_models.absolute_risk_context: These are relative odds, not an absolute or lifetime risk. Gout is multifactorial and most people carrying this genotype never develop gout. This is a risk modifier, not a diagnosis.
genotype_models.finding_text: ABCG2 Q141K homozygous (rs2231142 {genotype}) — both copies carry the urate-transporter risk allele, which reduces urate excretion. {odds_ratio}. {absolute_risk}

odds_ratio_by_ancestry.EAS: Gout odds ratio ~2.10 for one Q141K risk allele, using Asian-ancestry pooled genotype estimates
odds_ratio_by_ancestry.AFR: African-ancestry evidence is sparse; a meta-analysis estimated ~1.76x gout odds per Q141K risk allele
odds_ratio_by_ancestry.default: Gout odds ratio ~1.64 for one Q141K risk allele, using Caucasian/European pooled genotype estimates

genotype_models.absolute_risk_context: These are relative odds, not an absolute or lifetime risk. Gout is multifactorial and most people carrying one risk allele never develop gout. This is a risk modifier, not a diagnosis.
genotype_models.finding_text: ABCG2 Q141K heterozygous (rs2231142 {genotype}) — you carry one urate-transporter risk allele, which reduces urate excretion. {odds_ratio}. {absolute_risk}

SLC2A9 urate-raising genotype (rs13129697 {genotype}) — both copies carry the common urate-raising allele at SLC2A9, the largest common serum-urate gene. The per-person effect is modest and the alternative allele is urate-lowering. This describes a slight genetic tendency toward higher serum urate, not gout on its own, and is not a diagnosis.

SLC2A9 urate-raising allele, one copy (rs13129697 {genotype}) — you carry a single copy of the common urate-raising allele at SLC2A9, the largest common serum-urate gene. SLC2A9's effect on serum urate is per-allele (additive), so one copy contributes roughly half the shift of two; the per-person effect is small and the alternative allele is urate-lowering. This describes a slight genetic tendency toward higher serum urate, not gout on its own, and is not a diagnosis.
